We are going to grow revenue by fifteen percent.
We are launching the new line in the third quarter.
Izabel, thank you. Let us look at the road ahead. What is the plan for the third quarter?
Thank you, Andy. We are launching the new wireless sensor line in Q3. The timeline is set, and the first phase kicks off in early April.
Good. And the numbers? What does your forecast look like?
Our forecast is strong. We are going to grow revenue by about fifteen percent, and the projection for the new line alone is very promising.
Encouraging. Are you planning to invest more in research?
Yes. We are going to allocate more budget to research, so the pipeline stays full. I will send you the full roadmap after the call.
Perfect. One more thing. The partners will ask about risk. How are you preparing?
I anticipated that. We always keep a contingency: if a supplier is late, we have a backup plan ready. And I am meeting the regional partners next Tuesday to agree on the scope.
We are launching the new sensor line in the third quarter.
We are going to grow revenue by about fifteen percent.
The first phase kicks off in early April.
I will send you the full roadmap after the call.
We always keep a contingency in case a supplier is late.

Hi Izabel, this is Nathan from the board, with a quick look at the road ahead before Thursday. Here is the plan. In the third quarter, we are launching the new wireless sensor line. That decision is already made, and the timeline is set. The first phase kicks off in early April, and the first shipment is leaving the plant on the twelfth. On the numbers, our forecast is strong. We are going to grow revenue by about fifteen percent, and the projection for the new line alone is very promising. We have also decided to allocate more budget to research, so the pipeline stays full. There is one open question. The board is going to ask you about hiring, so anticipate it and come prepared. My advice is simple. Come with a clear roadmap, and I am sure you will do brilliantly. Talk on Thursday.

Good morning, Izabel. It is Sofia, with a few notes before the call. I have been thinking about the road ahead, and here is where I stand. We are meeting the regional partners next Tuesday. That is fixed, so the scope of the new market is going to be the first item. My own forecast is cautious but positive. I think demand will hold, and the projection for the second half looks solid. We are going to run a small pilot first, and if the numbers are good, we will scale from there. I have already set a target of twelve new accounts by December, and I am allocating two people to it full time. One more thing. Always keep a contingency. If a supplier is late, we need a backup plan ready. I will send you my roadmap tonight. Call me anytime.
